sanskrit:
तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता ।
भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ॥
transliteration: tejaḥ kṣamā dhṛtiḥ śaucam adroho nāti-mānitā | bhavanti sampadaṃ daivīm abhijātasya bhārata ||
literal_meaning: Vigor, forgiveness, fortitude, cleanliness, freedom from envy, absence of hankering for honor—these become the divine endowments of one born of godly nature, O Bharata.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists the final cluster of six traits that mark a psychologically integrated, spiritually aligned personality: radiant energy without arrogance, emotional regulation through forgiveness, stress resilience, somatic and moral hygiene, non-comparative self-worth, and humility that needs no external applause.
psychological_state: Post-therapeutic integration: ego-strength balanced by humility, low contamination of self-worth by social comparison, and high distress tolerance.
trigger_scenario: After receiving critical feedback in a performance review, you feel heat in your chest (tejas) but pause, breathe, and respond with curiosity instead of defensiveness—later noticing you never checked how many ‘likes’ your rebuttal got.
anchor_text: Live with quiet strength, clean heart, and no need for praise.
